sections.context: src/ (the flat python dev tree from `dizzy gen`) and lib/ (per-runtime, per-element packages from `dizzy lib`) are generated independently and don't share code. dizzy lib re-emits its own stubs — some degenerate (the query stub is `def list_signatures() -> None`, dropping input/context/output) — and lib element packages import gen_def/gen_int without declaring them as dependencies, so they aren't self-contained. lib/ is the path to multi-language support, so src/ should be removed and lib/ made to carry the implementations. Decided layout (python-uv): gen_def and gen_int become installable uv workspace packages living under lib/python-uv/, and each element package declares them as workspace dependencies. No top-level gen_def/gen_int/src remain.
sections.approach: Make lib/ the single home for generated code and implementations. (1) Relocate python type generation so `dizzy gen` writes gen_def/ and gen_int/ as installable packages under lib/python-uv/ (symmetric with rust-cargo/typescript-npm which already vendor gen_def under lib/<runtime>/), and stop emitting src/. (2) Make python-uv element packages (procedure/policy/query/projection) carry real-signature implementation stubs — reusing the correct render_src_* signatures and fixing the degenerate query stub — and declare gen_def/gen_int as workspace dependencies in their pyproject.toml. The workspace manifest lists gen_def, gen_int, and every element package as members with [tool.uv.sources] wiring. (3) Regenerate the guestbook example, migrating the existing src/ implementations into the lib/ element packages, and rewrite demo.py to import the installed packages. (4) Update tests/snapshots and docs. python-uv gets the full treatment; rust-cargo/typescript-npm are kept building but real-signature (protocol-derived) stubs for them are deferred to follow-up issues since they don't generate protocols/contexts yet.

alternatives.name: Vendor gen_def/gen_int copies into each element package
alternatives.rejected_because: More duplication; the workspace already provides a clean shared redistribution boundary.

alternatives.name: Keep gen_def/gen_int at the output-dir root and depend via path
alternatives.rejected_because: Rejected by design choice in favor of a fully self-contained per-runtime lib/ tree.

alternatives.name: Fold all type generation into `dizzy lib` and retire `dizzy gen`
alternatives.rejected_because: The issue scopes updating both gen and lib, and type compilation is a universal step distinct from runtime packaging.

steps.title: Relocate python type-gen under lib/python-uv/ and make gen_def/gen_int installable packages (linkml_runner paths, cli.gen, init_emitter, package-manifest emitters)
python-uv element packages carry real-signature impl stubs + declare gen_def/gen_int workspace deps; drop src/ generation (fix degenerate query stub, lib_python_uv, workspace manifest with sources)
Regenerate the guestbook example into lib-only layout and rewrite demo.py (migrate old src/ implementations into lib/ element packages, remove src/ and top-level gen_def/gen_int)
Update tests + regenerate snapshots (test_cli lib-layout/deps assertions, generator tests, .ambr) — `just test` and `just check` green
Update docs to lib-only flow (README, examples/README, examples/guestbook/README, dizzy docs_content.md)
Keep rust-cargo/typescript-npm building under the new lib flow; file follow-up issues for protocol-derived real-signature stubs in those runtimes

risks: Package import roots: making gen_def/gen_int importable as `gen_def`/`gen_int` while also being buildable packages may require a nested or src-layout; getting hatchling config + import paths consistent is fiddly.
demo.py wiring changes from sys.path hacks to installed-workspace imports; the example must still run end-to-end and print the expected guestbook output.
Snapshot churn will be large (paths move from src/ and gen_* to lib/python-uv/...); risk of rubber-stamping incorrect output during --snapshot-update.
rust/ts type-gen in `dizzy lib` writes gen_def under lib/<runtime>/; relocating python type-gen must not break the active-runtime detection or non-python paths.
acceptance: No dizzy command emits a src/ tree, and no top-level gen_def/ or gen_int/ is produced; gen_def and gen_int live under lib/python-uv/ as installable packages.
Every python-uv element package declares gen_def and gen_int as dependencies and has a real-signature stub (no `def list_signatures() -> None`).

Running `dizzy def && dizzy gen && dizzy lib` on the guestbook produces a self-contained lib/python-uv/ uv workspace that installs, and demo.py runs and prints the expected newest-first guestbook output.
`just test` and `just check` pass; docs (README, examples/README, guestbook/README, docs_content.md) describe the lib-only flow with no references to src/.

sections.context: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy simulate. Simulate executes a feature-file with no code generation and no deployment: each component activation is one narrow LLM call whose tools are synthesized from declared wiring only, and the harness — not the prompt — enforces every constraint. The product of a run is its findings list (missing slots, ambiguous descriptions, undecidable branches, unanswerable queries); the trace is secondary. This is the command that validates a design (and its prose/mermaid descriptions) the same evening it is sketched, before any code exists — the highest-excitement, highest-leverage demo of the DIZZY thesis that the design lives in the artifact, not in a context window.
sections.approach: Build the deterministic skeleton first and keep LLMs at the edge. (1) A phased single-thread scheduler (drain event queue, then command queue, repeat) over the feat-file wiring graph, driven by pluggable component executors — scripted/stub executors make the whole core testable in CI with no LLM. (2) The harness contract: per-activation tool synthesis from declared wiring (a procedure that may emit two event types gets exactly two emit tools; report_finding always present) plus schema validation that rejects nonconforming payloads. (3) --manual mode as the compatibility floor: prompt to stdout, JSON tool-call on stdin — this yields the first end-to-end level-0 run on the guestbook example; --exec and --provider are conveniences layered on the identical message protocol. (4) Fidelity levels as strictly increasing layers: 0 narrative (feat-file only), 1 slot-conformant payloads (requires authored def/ schemas), 2 stateful (one JSON blob per Model in the session file; projections propose mutations; queriers answer only from supplied state). (5) The session file is an append-only JSONL log — itself a DIZZY event stream — versioned from day one, enabling replay and branching (replay to step N, swap a modified feature-file, resume). New module under dizzy/src/dizzy/simulate/ with a `dizzy simulate` verb.

alternatives.name: One mega-prompt LLM simulating the whole system in a single context
alternatives.rejected_because: No enforcement, no termination control, and 'design in a context window' is the exact disease DIZZY cures.

alternatives.name: Provider-SDK-first integration (a vendor SDK as the only path)
alternatives.rejected_because: Agent compatibility is minimal by design; the manual/subprocess floor keeps the harness provider-agnostic and cheap models viable.

alternatives.name: Skip simulation; validate via generate + real implementations
alternatives.rejected_because: simulate's entire value is pre-codegen design validation.

alternatives.name: Exhaustive interleaving/race exploration
alternatives.rejected_because: Out of scope: the phased loop selects one legal interleaving (sequentially consistent best case); TLA+ generation from the same feature-file is future work.

steps.title: simulate core: wiring graph + phased event/command scheduler with scripted executors and step budget (no LLM)
simulate harness contract: per-activation tool synthesis from declared wiring (emit/dispatch/query + report_finding) and payload schema validation with rejection
simulate findings pipeline: report_finding capture, repetition/livelock detector, findings report as the run product (JSON + pretty)
simulate --manual mode: prompt rendering from component descriptions, JSON tool-call protocol on stdin; first end-to-end level-0 run on guestbook
simulate session file: versioned append-only JSONL run log (itself a DIZZY event stream) + deterministic replay
simulate branching: replay to step N, swap modified feature-file, resume (counterfactual design probes)
simulate --level 1: slot-conformant payloads validated against authored def/ schemas (gated on def/ presence)
simulate --level 2: stateful models — one JSON blob per Model in session; projections propose mutations; queriers answer only from supplied state
simulate --exec mode: subprocess protocol (prompt on stdin, JSON tool-call on stdout) for non-interactive agent CLIs
simulate --provider mode: direct API calls with config-selected model
simulate ship polish: flip docs/cli.md to shipped, cross-ref onboard/authoring pages, guestbook simulate walkthrough in examples/

risks: LLM payload nonconformance loops: rejection must re-prompt with the validation error under a bounded retry budget, then record a finding — never silently accept, never spin.
Level 1+ requires authored def/ schemas; a bare feat-file supports only level 0 — gate levels explicitly and report why, or users will read missing schemas as simulator bugs.
Livelock detector false positives on legitimate retry/fan-out patterns (same component, same message shape, N times) — make N configurable and emit a finding rather than hard-aborting.
The session file becomes a de-facto public contract once branching exists — version the format from the first commit, like the feat-file.
Scope creep toward race/concurrency checking — the phased loop proves one legal interleaving only; keep that boundary explicit in output and docs.
stdin-driven --manual mode is awkward under test — drive it with typer's CliRunner and piped fixtures so CI never needs a tty or an LLM.
acceptance: dizzy simulate <feat> --manual --level 0 runs the guestbook feature end-to-end: per-activation prompts rendered, JSON tool calls accepted on stdin, termination by quiescence or step budget, findings printed as JSON + pretty.
Emitting an undeclared event/command is structurally impossible (no tool synthesized); at level >= 1 a nonconforming payload is rejected and recorded as a finding, never silently accepted.
Session file is versioned append-only JSONL; branching works: replay to step N with a swapped feature-file and resume.
Level 2 keeps one JSON blob per Model in the session file; queriers answer only from supplied state; projections propose mutations through the harness.
--exec drives a non-interactive agent CLI through the same protocol as --manual; --provider makes direct API calls; both reuse the manual message protocol unchanged.
Scheduler, tool synthesis, validation, and detectors are covered by deterministic scripted-executor tests — no LLM call anywhere in CI; uv run pytest and ty check pass at baseline.
docs/cli.md § dizzy simulate flipped to shipped, amended with any spec refinements discovered during the build.
